title: GLYCINE
description: Glycine is a non-essential amino acid that serves as a key neurotransmitter and a precursor to glutathione and collagen. It acts as an inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain and a co-agonist for NMDA receptors, primarily used to enhance sleep quality, metabolic health, and physical recovery.

research.title: Glycine ingestion improves subjective sleep quality in human volunteers, correlating with polysomnographic changes
research.summary: Study finding that taking 3g of glycine before bed improves sleep quality and reduces daytime sleepiness by stabilizing sleep architecture.

research.title: The Sleep-Promoting and Hypothermic Effects of Glycine are Mediated by NMDA Receptors in the Suprachiasmatic Nucleus
research.summary: Research demonstrating that glycine promotes sleep by lowering core body temperature through increased peripheral blood flow via NMDA receptor activation.

research.title: An Update of the Promise of Glycine Supplementation for Enhancing Physical Performance and Recovery
research.summary: A review of how glycine aids in collagen synthesis and antioxidant protection, supporting muscle recovery and joint health in athletes.

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly report improved sleep quality and a sense of calm, with many noting its effectiveness as an affordable and transparent addition to a recovery stack.

effects.label: Sleep Quality:
effects.description: Highly effective at reducing 'sleep morning grogginess' and improving perceived restfulness.

effects.label: Physical Recovery:
effects.description: Users report reduced joint pain when taken consistently with collagen or protein.

effects.label: Onset:
effects.description: Sleep-promoting effects are typically felt within 30-60 minutes of ingestion.

dosage.label: Standard Sleep Dose:
dosage.description: 3 grams taken approximately one hour before bedtime is the most common and research-backed amount.

dosage.label: General Wellness:
dosage.description: 1-2 grams daily for general metabolic support.

dosage.label: Athletic Recovery:
dosage.description: 5-10 grams divided throughout the day frequently used by those with high training volumes.

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Upset:
sideEffects.description: High doses on an empty stomach may cause mild nausea or softening of stools.

sideEffects.label: Vivid Dreams:
sideEffects.description: Some users report an increase in dream intensity or frequency during the first week of use.

sideEffects.label: Daytime Drowsiness:
sideEffects.description: Rarely reported unless the dose is excessively high or taken late in the morning.

availability.label: Retail Availability:
availability.description: Widely available as an over-the-counter dietary supplement in powder and capsule form.

availability.label: Affordability:
availability.description: One of the most cost-effective supplements, often purchased in bulk powder due to its naturally sweet taste.
